title: ORDER BY clause ignored in 3-way join query
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE users (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL, age INT, created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
CREATE TABLE posts (id INT PRIMARY KEY, user_id INT, content VARCHAR(255), created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, FOREIGN KEY(user_id) REFERENCES users(id));

CREATE TABLE comments (id INT PRIMARY KEY, post_id INT, user_id INT, comment VARCHAR(255), created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, FOREIGN KEY(post_id) REFERENCES posts(id), FOREIGN KEY(user_id) REFERENCES users(id));
INSERT INTO users (name, age) VALUES ('Alice', 30), ('Bob', NULL), ('Charlie', 25);
SELECT u.name, COUNT(c.id) AS comment_count FROM users u LEFT JOIN posts p ON u.id = p.user_id LEFT JOIN comments c ON p.id = c.post_id GROUP BY u.id ORDER BY comment_count DESC;
key_bug_features: ORDER BY clause in 3-way join query
